meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Master Debugging for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome debugging hurdles with the Antigravity project template. A high-impact guide for students to master complex codebases using Claude Code and AI tools.
content.answer_block: To solve persistent debugging issues, utilize the Antigravity project template to enforce architectural discipline. By integrating Claude Code, you transform chaotic logic into structured, modular code. This workflow automates root-cause analysis, allowing you to bypass manual syntax hunting and focus on building scalable, industry-ready features for your student portfolio.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the silence of an empty IDE, mocking the hours you've spent chasing a phantom bug. You’ve built the feature, but the logic is tangled, the stack trace is a labyrinth, and the frustration is mounting. It’s the rite of passage every developer faces: the wall between 'it works' and 'it’s production-ready.'

But what if you didn't have to debug alone? The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural blueprint, turning the chaos of unmanaged code into a structured, predictable ecosystem. By pairing this template with the precision of Claude Code, you aren't just fixing bugs—you are engineering a system that resists them.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Blueprint
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure observability and clean separation of concerns from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity template with strict TypeScript configuration and modular error handling middleware.

steps.title: Automated Dependency Mapping
steps.description: Identify circular dependencies or bloated imports that often hide complex bugs in student projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project dependency graph and suggest refactoring for decoupled service layers.

steps.title: Context-Aware Traceability
steps.description: Inject logging hooks into your Antigravity template to surface silent failures before they crash your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement comprehensive logging middleware across all API routes to track execution flow and state changes.

steps.title: Hypothesis-Driven Debugging
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on specific logic blocks rather than guessing at potential fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Debug the authentication service by simulating edge cases for token expiration and invalid payload structures.

steps.title: Automated Regression Validation
steps.description: Ensure your fixes don't break existing features by running the Antigravity test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the newly refactored modules to ensure 90% code coverage and zero regressions.

pro_tips: Always define a 'root cause' variable in your comments before running a fix; it forces you to understand the logic before the AI writes the code.
Use the Antigravity 'hot-reload' mode during development to see state changes in real-time, reducing the need for manual console logs.
Treat your error logs as data; pipe them into Claude Code to identify patterns rather than looking at individual stack traces.
Modularize your code early; if a file exceeds 200 lines, use the Antigravity refactor tool to split it into domain-specific services.
Document your 'aha!' moments in a README.md 'troubleshooting' section—it doubles as a portfolio piece showing senior-level debugging skills.
content.next_steps: Once your project is stable, deploy it to a staging environment using the Antigravity CI/CD pipeline to practice professional DevOps workflows.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap to ship software that actually works. Stop fighting your code and start mastering it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
